title: RelayOS Series A Roadshow
decision: decide whether RelayOS has enough traction and technical defensibility for diligence
prompt:
Create an evidence-led seven-slide Series A roadshow for early-stage enterprise software investors deciding whether RelayOS merits diligence. Use only the attached operating metrics and product facts. Show the workflow problem, product transformation, traction, market wedge, defensible architecture, financing use, and a clear diligence decision. Include an editable traction chart, an editable system architecture diagram, a large honestly labeled product screenshot slot, and one restrained market comparison. Do not invent customers, market size, quotes, or growth rates.

Design direction: Use an evidence-led editorial direction: restrained surface treatment, decisive hierarchy, visible provenance, and an inspectable visual argument on every proof slide. Avoid card grids and decorative dashboards.

attachments.content:
metric,value,unit,source
paid_workspaces,84,workspaces,internal ledger
monthly_active_operators,1260,operators,product telemetry
weekly_workflows,18400,workflows,product telemetry
net_revenue_retention,118,percent,billing export
median_setup_time,19,minutes,onboarding telemetry

RelayOS coordinates approval-heavy operations. The product keeps proposals reviewable before mutation, records versioned receipts, and exposes adapters for CRM, ticketing, and document systems. No product screenshot was supplied to the benchmark.

designDirection.label: Evidence editorial
designDirection.instruction: Use an evidence-led editorial direction: restrained surface treatment, decisive hierarchy, visible provenance, and an inspectable visual argument on every proof slide. Avoid card grids and decorative dashboards.
